data.title: NFLX: Fairly Valued Amid Slowing Subscriber Growth, Structural Headwinds, and Risky WBD Acquisition Plans
data.description:
Jeffrey Wlodarczak, an analyst from Pivotal Research, reiterated the Hold rating on Netflix. The associated price target was lowered to $95.00.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jeffrey Wlodarczak has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Netflix’s growth profile, valuation, and competitive environment. He notes that while the company’s recent quarter modestly exceeded expectations on revenue and operating income and guidance for 2026 aligns with his prior outlook, subscriber numbers came in meaningfully below his forecast, with growth driven more by price hikes than by user additions. This has led him to cut his long-term subscriber projections and to apply a lower terminal EBITDA multiple in his valuation model, which together reduce his year‑end 2026 target price to $95 and limit upside from current levels.

At the same time, he sees structural pressures mounting as younger audiences increasingly favor short-form, free social platforms and as free ad-supported TV offerings gain traction among more price-sensitive households, both of which could weigh on Netflix’s long‑form streaming model over time. He also views Netflix’s planned, high-cost acquisition of WBD’s studio and streaming assets as a double-edged sword: it could solidify Netflix’s global leadership in premium content but introduces regulatory, execution, and pricing risks, along with the potential for a prolonged closing period and a higher ultimate purchase price. When combined with sizable content obligations, uncertainties around the ad-supported transition, reduced transparency from the cessation of subscriber reporting, and broader regulatory and M&A risks, these factors support his view that the stock is fairly valued, justifying a Hold rather than a Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, TipRanks – PerPlexity also downgraded the stock to a Hold with a $96.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
